credit: presentation taxonomy and no-invention rule: yairwein/backgammon-teacher (MIT)

rules.sentence: Your move gives them more shots than the best play does.
The best play hits; yours lets their checker stand.
The best play makes their entry from the bar costlier.
The best play closes another home-board point against them.
The best play tightens the blockade on their back checkers.
The best play keeps your rear point made; your move gives it up.
The best play makes an anchor in their board; yours leaves you without one.
The best play keeps your rear structure connected.
The best play sends more of their pips back than yours does.
The best play stays in contact where your move runs.
